description: Everyday objects, tools, and miscellaneous goods found in homes, shops, and dungeons.
items: Acorns
A palmful of glossy brown oak nuts rattling in a small leather pouch.
Adze
A curved blade lashed to a short haft, hollowing wood with each chopping stroke.
Alarm Bell
A small brass bell rigged to a trip cord, clanging with shrill urgency at the slightest tug.
Ale Barrel
An iron-hooped oak keg damp with condensation, sloshing with bitter brown ale.
Alembic
A bulbous still with a curving copper beak, green with verdigris at every seam.
Altar Cloth
Embroidered linen edged with gold thread, faintly stained with candle drippings.
Amber
A honey-gold lump of fossilized resin with a tiny insect trapped forever inside.
Animal Lead
A braided leather strap with a brass clip at one end, scored by teeth marks and pulling.
Animal Skull
A bleached fox skull with empty sockets, delicate as paper and white as chalk.
Antimony Rod
A dull metallic stick cool to the touch, leaving grey streaks on anything it scrapes.
Antler
A branching tine of shed deer horn, velvet-rough at the base and smooth at the tips.
Anvil
A massive iron block dished and scarred across its face, ringing under every blow.
Armor Polish
A jar of gritty paste smelling of vinegar, turning tarnished steel mirror-bright.
Arrow Fletchings
Trimmed goose feathers bundled with thread, ready to guide a shaft in flight.
Artifact
An ancient object of unknown origin, etched with indecipherable symbols and faintly warm to the touch.
Atlas
A heavy leather-bound volume of maps and charts, its pages foxed with age and creased at well-traveled routes.
Backgammon Board
An inlaid wooden case with bone counters and ivory dice, hinges creaking with age.
Backpack
A canvas rucksack with patched straps, shaped by the weight of long journeys.
Bag
A simple drawstring sack of rough cloth, shapeless and waiting to be filled.
Bagpipe
A leather bladder fitted with wooden drones and a chanter, wheezing even when not being played.
Balance Scale
Twin brass pans on a knife-edge fulcrum, trembling at the weight of a single grain.
Ball of Yarn
A tightly wound sphere of dyed wool trailing a loose thread from its center.
Band
A plain strip of hammered metal bent into a circle, unadorned and cool against the skin.
Bandage
A rolled strip of clean linen slightly yellowed at the edges, ready to bind a wound.
Bark
A rough chunk of stripped tree bark, fibrous on one side and smooth on the other.
Barrel
An oak cask bound with rust-spotted iron hoops, damp and smelling of old wine.
Basket
A shallow open vessel of woven reed, light enough to carry on the hip and sturdy underfoot.
Beacon
An iron brazier mounted on a tall pole, its oil-soaked fuel bundle ready to blaze at a spark.
Bedroll
A tightly cinched bundle of wool blanket and oiled canvas, smelling of campfire.
Beeswax
A warm-toned block scored with honeycomb texture, faintly sweet-smelling.
Bell
A palm-sized bronze chime whose clear tone lingers long after the strike fades.
Bellows
Cracked leather lungs on wooden paddles, wheezing hot air into a forge's coals.
Belt
A wide leather strap scored with old buckle marks, darkened by sweat and weather.
Belt Knife
A short utility blade in a hip sheath, its wooden grip polished dark by daily use.
Bent Spoon
A pewter utensil twisted nearly backward, useless for eating but oddly fascinating.
Bird Nest
A cup of woven twigs and dried moss, abandoned and brittle with age.
Bit
A curved iron mouthpiece for a horse's bridle, worn smooth where teeth have gnawed it.
Blacksmith's Hammer
A heavy iron head on a sweat-stained oak handle, its face pocked with use.
Blanket
A thick wool rectangle scratchy against bare skin, mothball-scented but warm.
Blessed Amulet
A hammered copper disc inscribed with prayers so tiny they blur without a lens.
Blessing Oil
A stoppered vial of golden liquid smelling of myrrh and pressed olives.
Blotting Sand
Fine white powder in a perforated shaker, dusting fresh ink dry in seconds.
Bone
A cleaned and sun-bleached fragment of animal bone, light and hollow-sounding when tapped.
Bone Dice
Yellowed knucklebone cubes carved with pips, clacking like teeth when thrown.
Book
A leather-bound volume with a cracked spine, its pages dense with faded handwritten text.
Bookstand
An oak lectern darkened by lamp soot, its ledge grooved from heavy manuscript spines.
Boots
Thick-soled leather reaching mid-calf, mud-caked and creased at the ankles.
Bottle
A thick glass vessel with a narrow neck and a cork stopper, tinted green by its contents.
Bow String
A twist of waxed linen cord, stiff with tension and fraying where it loops the nock.
Bowl
A turned wooden bowl with a smooth interior, darkened by years of use and oily hands.
Box
A small hinged wooden case with a brass latch, scuffed at the corners from rough handling.
Bracer
A stiff leather forearm guard scored by bowstring snaps, buckled tight.
Brand
A long iron rod ending in a shaped stamp, its head blackened from being heated cherry-red.
Brazier
A squat iron fire bowl on clawed feet, its rim warped by intense and repeated heat.
Bread Loaf
A dense round of dark rye with a cracked crust, still faintly warm inside.
Brewing Supples
A bundled kit of hops, yeast cakes, and muslin straining cloth, smelling of fermentation.
Brick
A rough clay block fired orange-red, chipped at one corner and gritty to the touch.
Brittle Parchment
An ancient calfskin sheet yellowed and flaking, crumbling to dust at the folds.
Broken Arrow
A splintered ash shaft snapped at the midpoint, its iron head canted at a useless angle.
Broken Hourglass
A cracked glass bulb spilling fine sand from its wooden frame, time leaking away.
Broom
A bundle of stiff birch twigs bound to a worn handle, bristles splayed from years of sweeping.
Brush
A flat wooden paddle set with coarse bristles, stained with paint or dust depending on its last use.
Bucket
A staved wooden pail banded with iron, its rope handle frayed from hauling.
Burnt Book
A leather-bound volume charred black at the edges, its inner pages still barely legible.
Butter
A pale yellow block wrapped in damp muslin, soft and faintly sour-sweet.
Butterfly Wing
An iridescent membrane patterned in orange and black, delicate as a breath could shatter it.
Candle
A pale taper of beeswax or tallow, its wick blackened and curling from a previous burning.
Cane
A slim rod of dark lacquered wood with a curved handle, tapping sharply on cobblestones.
Canteen
A dented metal flask fitted with a screw cap, sloshing with lukewarm water.
Canvas
A folded sheet of heavy unbleached cloth, stiff with sizing and ready for brush or shelter.
Canvas Tarp
A stiff oiled sheet creased from folding, patched in two places with darker cloth.
Cards
A deck of illustrated pasteboard trimmed with gilt edges, slightly sticky from humid hands.
Carpet
A thick woven rug patterned in faded reds and golds, heavy enough to muffle footsteps.
Cart
A two-wheeled wooden frame with iron-rimmed wheels, creaking and spattered with dried mud.
Castanets
Paired chestnut shells clacking sharp and bright, hollowed smooth on the inside.
Cauldron
A heavy cast-iron pot with a bail handle, blackened inside and out by decades over open flame.
Censer
A pierced bronze globe swinging on blackened chains, leaking perfumed haze.
Ceramic Jug
A glazed vessel cool to the touch, stoppered with cork and smelling faintly of ale.
Chainmail Repair Kit
A pouch of loose iron rings, pliers, and a riveting punch — a field armorer's essentials.
Chains
Heavy iron links joined in a rattling length, cold to the touch and darkened with rust.
Chair
A straight-backed wooden seat with turned legs, its joints loose and creaking under weight.
Chalice
A stemmed goblet of tarnished silver, its bowl engraved with twining vines and faded gilt.
Chalk
A dry white mineral stick that squeaks against slate and crumbles under pressure.
Chamber Pot
Chipped ceramic with a faded floral glaze, best left unexamined too closely.
Charcoal
Lightweight black lumps crumbling at a touch, burning hotter than the wood they came from.
Charcoal Stick
A brittle black twig leaving smudgy marks on anything it touches.
Cheese Wheel
A cloth-wrapped disc of aged cheddar, sharp-smelling and crumbling at the rind.
Cherry blossom
A delicate spray of pale pink petals on a slender twig, bruising brown at the faintest touch.
Chess Set
Carved boxwood and ebony pieces in a hinged board, worn smooth at the tops.
Chest
An iron-banded wooden box with a heavy lid, scarred by pry marks and blackened at the lock.
Chimes
Suspended tubes of hammered bronze that ring in overlapping tones when stirred by the wind.
Chipped Goblet
A pewter cup missing a crescent from its rim, its base dented from a hard fall.
Chisel
A flat-edged steel blade set in a battered handle, its cutting edge nicked and resharpened many times.
Chrysanthemum
A dense globe of tightly layered petals in deep gold, its stem stiff and slightly sticky.
Clasp
A small metal fastener with a spring catch, tarnished but still clicking shut with precision.
Clay
A damp grey lump wrapped in wet rags, cool and yielding under the thumb.
Clay Amphora
A tall two-handled vessel sealed with pitch, its terracotta surface chalky and cool.
Clay Cooking Pot
Thick-walled earthenware blackened underneath, still smelling of last night's stew.
Cloak Pin
A cast bronze brooch shaped like a coiled serpent, its clasp still sharp.
Clockwork Mouse
A palm-sized brass automaton on tiny wheels, skittering forward when its key is wound.
Cloth
A folded length of undyed linen, rough-woven and smelling faintly of the loom's oil.
Cloth Bag
A simple linen sack cinched with a drawstring, shapeless until something fills it.
Coin
A single stamped disc of copper or silver, its face worn smooth by countless palms.
Coin Pouch
A palm-sized leather purse with a brass snap, bulging unevenly with mixed currency.
Coin Purse
A drawstring leather pouch soft with wear, jingling faintly with every step.
Coins
A loose handful of mixed currency — copper, silver, and the rare glint of gold — clinking together.
Comb
A flat piece of carved horn with fine teeth, warm from a pocket and slightly greasy.
Compass
A brass case holding a trembling magnetic needle floating on a jeweled pivot.
Contract
A folded sheet of heavy parchment bearing dense script, sealed with a wax impression at the bottom.
Copper Wire
A spool of bright-drawn wire tarnishing green at the cut ends.
Coral Branch
A forked calcium skeleton bleached white, light as balsa and brittle as old bone.
Cord
A thin braided length of waxed cotton, supple and strong enough for binding or lashing.
Corroded Coin
A copper disc encrusted in green patina, its stamped face completely unreadable.
Cracked Mirror
A palm-sized silver glass split by a web of fractures, reflecting a dozen fragmented faces.
Cracked Pottery
A clay jar split down one side and bound with wire, still holding its contents — barely.
Crossbow Bolt
A stubby iron-tipped shaft with stiff leather vanes, shorter and heavier than an arrow.
Crucible
A fire-glazed clay cup stained with metallic residue, chipped at the pouring lip.
Crucible Tongs
Long iron pincers with heat-blackened tips, their spring stiff from forge scale.
Crutch
A padded wooden brace fitted under the arm, its shaft worn bright where hands have gripped.
Crystal
A faceted quartz shard refracting light into pale rainbows across nearby surfaces.
Crystal Fragment
A translucent quartz shard with natural hexagonal faces, cold and glass-sharp at the edges.
Cube
A solid block of polished stone or dark wood, its six faces perfectly smooth and featureless.
Daffodil
A trumpet-shaped bloom of bright yellow nodding on a slender green stem, cheerful and fleeting.
Dagger Sheath
A slim leather sleeve stitched for a hidden blade, its mouth stiffened with bone.
Daisy
A small white flower with a sunny yellow center, its petals closing shyly at dusk.
Dancing Skeleton
Articulated ivory bones on silk threads, capering with an eerily lifelike grace.
Dented Helmet
A steel cap bearing a deep concave dent above the brow — someone survived this blow.
Diary
A palm-sized book bound in soft leather, its pages filled with cramped personal handwriting.
Dice
Carved bone cubes yellowed by oily fingers, their pip-holes stained with black ink.
Dice Set
Six bone cubes rattling in a leather cup, their pips stained dark with ink.
Diorama
A miniature scene crafted inside a shallow box, painted figures frozen in a moment of tiny drama.
Distillation Flask
A pear-shaped glass vessel with a long tapered neck, clouded by chemical deposits.
Doll
A stitched cloth figure with button eyes and yarn hair, loved into softness by small hands.
Dowel
A smooth round rod of hardwood, straight and featureless, useful for a hundred small repairs.
Dragon's Blood Resin
Dark crimson granules smelling of pine and iron, brittle as old glass.
Draw Knife
Two-handled shaving blade with bark curls still clinging to its beveled edge.
Dream Catcher
A willow hoop laced with sinew webbing and trailing bedraggled feathers.
Dried Fish
Salt-crusted fillets stiff as wood, pungent but preserving well in any weather.
Dried Fruit
Leathery slices of apple and apricot, intensely sweet and chewy with concentrated sugar.
Driftwood
A salt-smoothed timber plank grey with age, its grain raised like weathered rope.
Drill
A bow-driven auger with a hardened steel bit, boring clean holes with each pull of the cord.
Drum
A goatskin head stretched over a cedar frame, booming deep when struck by palm.
Drums
A matched pair of skin-topped barrels braced between the knees, resonating deep with each strike.
Dulcimer
A trapezoidal box of polished walnut strung with fine wire, ringing sweetly when struck with small hammers.
Dust
A pouch of fine grey powder, gritty between the fingers and faintly metallic in smell.
Dye
A stoppered vial of concentrated liquid pigment, staining anything it touches for days.
Dye Bath
A stained wooden tub of deep crimson liquid, its fumes sharp with mordant.
Dye Powder
A pouch of vivid indigo dust that stains fingers blue for days.
Echo Stone
A hollow geode rattling with loose crystals inside, producing eerie whispers when shaken.
Egg
A speckled brown shell still warm from the nest, fragile and surprisingly heavy.
Fan
A folding screen of painted paper or silk on bamboo ribs, stirring a gentle breeze when flicked open.
Feathers
Exotic plumes in iridescent blues and greens, bundled with a linen tie.
Fiddle
A spruce-topped instrument darkened by rosin dust, its horsehair bow fraying at the tip.
Figurine
A hand-carved wooden figure no taller than a thumb, its painted features worn soft by handling.
File
A rough steel bar crosshatched with teeth, dusty with fine iron filings.
Fishing Line
A coil of waxed gut wound on a wooden spool, two barbed hooks tucked inside.
Fishing Pole
A long flexible rod of split bamboo with a line guide at the tip, light and whippy.
Flask
A curved metal vessel sized to fit a hip pocket, its cap chained to the neck to prevent loss.
Flint and Steel
A chipped flint shard and a curved steel striker, nestled with charcloth in a leather pouch.
Flour
A cloth sack of fine-ground wheat, dusty white and silky between the fingers.
Flour Sack
A dusty burlap bag sagging with the last remnants of milled grain.
Flute
A slender boxwood tube with silver keys, breathy and sweet in the upper register.
Flux Powder
A jar of grainy white borax that fizzes and pops when sprinkled on molten metal.
Fortune Cookie
A folded pastry shell brittle and sweet, concealing a slip of cryptic prophecy.
Fountain
A stone basin fed by a carved spout, its water pooling green with moss and old copper coins.
Four-Leaf Clover
A tiny green specimen pressed flat under glass, each leaf perfectly symmetrical.
Frayed Rope
Hemp cordage unraveling into a spray of loose fibers, barely holding together.
Funnel
A copper cone patinated green inside, its narrow spout dented from careless handling.
Fur Pelt
A thick winter hide tanned supple, its guard hairs still glossy and dense.
Gardenia
A waxy white blossom with an overpowering sweet fragrance, bruising to cream at the slightest pressure.
Gauntlet
An articulated steel glove lined with leather, scuffed bright at every knuckle.
Gauze
A sheer roll of loosely woven cotton, soft and absorbent for dressing wounds.
Gem
A rough-cut precious stone catching light in its cloudy facets, small enough to hide in a fist.
Gemstone
A cut stone no bigger than a thumbnail, flashing deep color when tilted to the light.
Gimlet
A spiral steel bit with a cross-handle, biting clean holes through seasoned timber.
Glass
A clear drinking vessel of blown glass, thin-walled and ringing faintly when tapped.
Glass Vial
A thumb-sized tube of blown glass, thin-walled and stoppered with wax.
Gloves
Supple calfskin fitted close to the hand, darkened at the fingertips from use.
Glue Pot
A crusty iron vessel of hide glue, reheated so often its rim is layered with drips.
Gold Coin
A thick disc stamped with a crowned profile, its rim clipped slightly short.
Gong
A wide disc of hammered bronze suspended from a wooden frame, resonating with a deep, shimmering tone.
Grain Sack
A burlap bag bulging with dry barley, tied shut with a knotted cord.
Grappling Hook
A three-pronged iron anchor scored bright at the tips from biting into stone.
Grinder
A hand-cranked mill of cast iron with grooved stones inside, crushing grain or spice to fine powder.
Grindstone
A large sandstone wheel grooved by years of spinning against blade edges.
Hair Ribbon
A length of silk dyed deep crimson, slightly frayed where it has been knotted.
Hand Mirror
A disc of polished steel in a cracked wooden frame, reflecting only a dim likeness.
Hand Saw
Toothed steel in a worn beech frame, its blade faintly stained with pine resin.
Handcuffs
Hinged iron cuffs joined by a short chain, clicking shut with a ratcheted finality.
Handkerchief
A square of fine linen edged with embroidery, folded crisp and scented with lavender.
Handle
A turned wooden grip shaped to the hand, drilled and ready to receive a tool head or blade.
Hard Biscuit
Twice-baked hardtack dense enough to crack a tooth, lasting months without spoiling.
Harp
A graceful maple frame strung with brass wire, each string humming at a touch.
Hat
A wide-brimmed felt hat stained with rain marks and sweat along the inner band.
Helmet Liner
A quilted linen cap stained with sweat, padded thick to cushion blows.
Herb Jar
A ceramic pot sealed with wax, its label written in a cramped alchemical shorthand.
Herb Pouch
A drawstring cloth bag exhaling rosemary and sage each time it is opened.
Herbal Tea Pouch
A small cloth sachet packed with dried chamomile and mint, releasing fragrance when steeped.
Herbs
A tied bundle of dried rosemary and thyme, fragrant enough to scent a whole room.
Hibiscus
A broad trumpet of crimson petals with a prominent stamen, wilting quickly once plucked.
Hinges
Iron leaves joined by a pin, rust-spotted but still swinging freely.
Holy Symbol
A tarnished silver pendant shaped in sacred geometry, warm against the chest.
Holy Water
A crystal vial of blessed liquid catching light with an almost luminous shimmer.
Honey Comb
A dripping slab of beeswax hexagons oozing thick golden syrup.
Honey Pot
A squat clay jar sealed with beeswax, golden sweetness oozing from a hairline crack.
Hook
A curved iron spike with a flattened eye for hanging, rough-forged and sharp at the point.
Horn
A curved ram's horn polished to a golden sheen, bellowing low when blown.
Horseshoe
A cold-forged iron arc still bearing nail holes, hung open-end-up for luck.
Hourglass
Two glass bulbs joined at a narrow throat, fine white sand whispering between them in a steady stream.
Hydrangea
A rounded cluster of papery blue petals crowning a thick green stem, shifting hue with the soil.
Incense
A dense block of compressed resin and dried herbs, releasing thick perfumed smoke when lit.
Incense Stick
A slender fragrant rod trailing grey smoke that curls and sweetens still air.
Ink Bottle
A squat glass vessel of iron-gall black, its cork crusted with dried drips.
Ink Pen
A steel-nibbed pen fitted into a slender wooden holder, its point split for ink flow.
Ink Pot
A squat ceramic well with a hinged lid, its interior permanently stained indigo.
Instrument
A portable musical device of wood and gut string, scuffed from travel and warm-toned from age.
Iron Gall Ink
A dark brown liquid that deepens to permanent black as it dries on the page.
Iron Kettle
A heavy, soot-crusted cauldron that clangs with a deep, hollow ring.
Iron Ladle
Long-handled and fire-blackened, its bowl warped slightly from years of scraping pot bottoms.
Iron Ore
A rough chunk of rust-streaked rock, dense and magnetic at its dark core.
Iron Strongbox
A riveted steel chest heavy enough to anchor itself, its padlock thick as a fist.
Ivory Carving
A palm-sized figurine yellowed with age, its details worn soft by handling.
Jar
A wide-mouthed ceramic vessel with a cork lid, cloudy with residue from its last contents.
Jasmine
Tiny star-shaped white blossoms on a trailing vine, their perfume intensifying after dark.
Jerky
Thin strips of peppered venison dried to a tough, salty chew.
Jewelry Box
A small rosewood case lined with faded velvet, its brass latch tarnished green.
Journal
A stitched notebook of blank or half-filled pages, its cover soft from being carried everywhere.
Juggling Balls
Sand-filled leather spheres dyed in fading primary colors, soft from countless catches.
Kaleidoscope
A brass tube rattling with colored glass shards, painting shifting mandalas through the eyepiece.
Key
A heavy iron key with square-cut teeth, cold in the palm and darkened with age.
Knucklebones
Polished sheep anklebones clattering like small stones, nicked from years of throwing.
Ladder
A rough-hewn assembly of rungs pegged into two long rails, wobbling slightly under weight.
Lamp
A brass reservoir with a cotton wick, casting a steady golden glow when filled with oil.
Lantern
A battered tin frame with glass panes and a sooty chimney, throwing jittery shadows.
Lavender
Slender purple flower spikes on grey-green stems, filling the air with a calming herbal scent.
Leaky Waterskin
A punctured leather bladder weeping a slow dark stain wherever it rests.
Leash
A long strip of braided leather with a looped handle, worn supple from daily walks.
Leather
A flat piece of tanned hide, supple and rich brown, smelling of the curing process.
Leather Awl
A needle-sharp steel point set into a round walnut grip, scored by calloused fingers.
Leather Satchel
A supple cowhide bag darkened by rain, its brass buckle tarnished to a dull green.
Leather Scraps
Irregular offcuts of tanned hide, still supple enough for patching and stitching.
Ledger
A tall narrow book of ruled pages filled with columns of numbers, its binding cracking at the spine.
Lens
A single disc of ground glass in a brass frame, magnifying or distorting whatever lies beneath.
Letter
A folded sheet of parchment sealed with wax, its contents private until the seal is broken.
Lever
A stout iron bar flattened at one end for prying, cold and heavy in the grip.
Lichen
Papery crusts of grey-green growth scraped from old bark, dry and faintly bitter.
Lilac
Conical clusters of tiny lavender blossoms on woody stems, breathing a sweet spring fragrance.
Lily
A tall stem crowned with a wide trumpet bloom, its white petals dusted with orange pollen.
Linseed Oil
A corked bottle of amber liquid leaving a slick film on anything it touches.
Lock
A heavy iron padlock with a keyhole in its face, tarnished and clicking stubbornly.
Lock Mechanism
An intricate brass assembly of tumblers and springs, clicking with precise resistance.
Lodestone
A rough black rock bristling with iron filings, tugging faintly toward anything metal.
Lotion
A corked bottle of thick cream scented with herbs, soothing and slightly greasy on the skin.
Lucky Rabbit's Foot
A dried paw on a tarnished chain, its fur matted and faintly unsettling.
Lure
A carved and painted wooden minnow fitted with a barbed hook, trailing a bright feather.
Lute
A pear-shaped body of polished rosewood with gut strings, warm-toned and resonant.
Lye Solution
Caustic liquid in a tightly stoppered bottle, sharp enough to sting the nostrils from a pace away.
Lyre
A U-shaped frame of polished wood strung with gut, small enough to cradle in one arm.
Mace Head
A flanged iron ball drilled for a haft, brutally heavy in the palm.
Magic 8-Ball
A heavy black sphere with a murky window, its floating die surfacing vague answers.
Magnifying Glass
A thick convex lens in a tarnished brass frame, scratched but still sharp.
Magnifying Lens
A thick glass disc in a hinged brass mount, smudged with fingerprints and dust.
Mallet
A dense hardwood head on a turned handle, its striking face dented soft with use.
Manacles
Heavy iron wrist cuffs linked by a short chain, their lock mechanism crude but effective.
Mandolin
A teardrop-bodied instrument with paired metal strings, bright and ringing under a quick pick.
Map
A sheet of parchment inked with coastlines, roads, and landmarks, folded soft along its creases.
Map Case
A waxed leather cylinder scuffed and water-stained, its brass cap threaded tight.
Marble
A small glass sphere swirled with color inside, cool and perfectly round in the palm.
Marigold
A ruffled bloom of deep orange with a pungent earthy scent, its stem slightly sticky.
Marking Gauge
An oak beam with a brass thumbscrew and a pin that scratches clean parallel lines.
Match
A thin wooden splint tipped with sulfur, flaring to life with a sharp scrape.
Matches
A small box of sulfur-tipped wooden splints, rattling faintly and smelling of brimstone.
Measuring Spoon
A set of tiny brass scoops on a ring, each etched with precise volume marks.
Mechanical Bird
A gilded tin songbird perched in a wire cage, trilling when its spring is wound.
Medicine Bag
A roll of waxed canvas holding glass vials, bronze forceps, and linen bandages.
Meditation Mat
A tightly woven reed mat worn thin where knees have pressed for years.
Melted Candle
A collapsed puddle of tallow frozen mid-drip over the edge of a shelf.
Mercury
Liquid silver pooling restlessly inside a sealed glass sphere, impossibly heavy for its size.
Mess Kit
A nested set of tin plate, cup, and utensils that clatter together in a canvas roll.
Message in Bottle
A salt-crusted glass vessel corked tight around a scroll of water-stained parchment.
Metal Shavings
Bright curls of brass and iron swept from a workbench, sharp-edged and glinting.
Milk
A ceramic pitcher of fresh cream-topped milk, cool from the cellar.
Mirror
A flat disc of polished steel or silver in a simple frame, reflecting a clear but slightly warped image.
Monocle
A single round lens in a thin brass frame, clipped to a fine chain for the vest pocket.
Monster Part
A severed claw, fang, or scale from an unknown beast, leathery and reeking of something feral.
Mood Ring
A thin silver band set with a stone that shifts from blue to amber with body heat.
Mortar and Pestle
A heavy marble bowl and grinding stone dusted with strange-colored residue.
Moss
A handful of soft sphagnum still damp from the forest floor, springy and green.
Mug
A thick stoneware cup with a sturdy handle, chipped at the rim and stained brown inside.
Mushroom
A dried bracket fungus tough as leather, faintly earthy and useful in alchemy.
Music Box
A lacquered wooden case that plays a tinkling melody when its tiny crank is turned.
Nail
A single hand-forged iron spike, slightly bent and rough at the head.
Nails
A handful of hand-forged iron spikes, each one slightly different in size.
Needle
A slender steel spike with a tiny eye, sharp enough to pierce leather and fine enough for silk.
Net
Knotted hemp mesh weighted with lead sinkers, smelling of river mud and fish.
Nuts
A pouch of mixed hazelnuts and walnuts, still dusty from their shells.
Offering Bowl
A shallow ceramic dish glazed midnight blue, chipped where coins have been tossed in.
Oil
A stoppered flask of viscous liquid that coats the fingers and smells faintly of earth.
Oil Flask
A stoppered clay bottle sloshing with lamp fuel, its neck greasy from dripping.
Oil Lamp
A small ceramic dish trailing a blackened wick, its belly slick with olive oil.
Orb
A smooth sphere of dark glass or polished stone, heavy in the hand and faintly reflective.
Orchid
An exotic bloom of violet and white with spotted petals, delicate and otherworldly on its arching stem.
Pack
A large leather rucksack with fraying shoulder straps, shaped by years of heavy loads.
Paddle
A flat-bladed wooden oar with a rounded grip, its face worn smooth from pushing through water.
Paintbrush
A slender wooden handle tipped with a tuft of soft bristle, its ferrule crusted with dried pigment.
Painting
A framed canvas layered with oil paint, its subject faded but still vivid in the highlights.
Paper Scraps
Rough rag-pulp sheets with uneven edges, cheaper and coarser than proper parchment.
Parchment Sheet
Scraped calfskin stretched thin and chalky-white, faintly translucent at the edges.
Paste
A thick jar of sticky compound smelling of flour and resin, useful for mending and sealing.
Pearl
